BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Personal monthly budget vs. take-home modeling. Looking at savings runway against the Lakefront Half Marathon prep block, the October NOLA trip, and the consistent monthly Great Lakes auto-pay. Goal: confirm we still hit the $20,000 Ally HYS target by January 2027.

CurrentState.Note: Rough monthly view. Actual surplus varies with race entries and family travel.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Monthly surplus is real but thin (~$917). Largest sensitivity is dining-out, which already self-limits during trial periods. The Great Lakes auto-pay is the hard fixed cost — it does not flex. Goal of $20,000 in savings by January 2027 is on track with the current $200/month auto-transfer plus periodic windfalls (CLE honoraria, tax refund).
ActionItems: Hold the $200/month Ally auto-transfer through Q3.
Re-check after October trials wrap to confirm trial-week dining savings landed.
Confirm tax refund routing before April (push to Ally, not checking).
